body_txt: This character tries to avoid wearing shoes whenever possible. The reasons for this vary greatly, some of which are common enough to be tropes of their own:  This is also sometimes a sign of youthful innocence or vulnerability, and many of the characters who demonstrate this trope are either children or childlike. The lack of shoes may also be used as an indication of untamed ruggedness. This trope is often used in visual media as an excuse to show frequent close-up shots of bare feet, usually due to Fanservice or Author Appeal (or both if you're Quentin Tarantino or Joss Whedon ), which may explain why female barefooters outnumber males practically 2-1, or why their feet are very rarely visibly dirty or calloused . Downplayed, but still broadly applicable, if a character has a notable preference for sandals or flip-flops, particularly in circumstances when others usually put on closed shoes or boots (like in cold weather or on formal occasions); this version is often used to still convey the essence of the trope while sidestepping questions of practicality. Does not apply if none of the characters wear shoes and the work portrays a society or setting where shoes don't even exist. Someone who's frequently shown barefoot is not necessarily this trope depending on the context. Characters who lost their shoes for reasons beyond their control— Barefoot Captives , Barefoot Poverty , stranded on a desert island, etc.—don't apply unless the habit sticks after their circumstances improve. This trope also doesn't apply if someone is only barefoot when expected, like if they just got out of bed, are at the beach or swimming pool, or wearing sandals on a hot day. Lastly, most web video creators film their videos at home; simply seeing their feet once in awhile is not enough for this trope unless they specifically acknowledge a preference for going barefoot or it's an obvious character quirk. A list of barefooters (people who prefer going barefoot in Real Life ) is also available on Wikipedia .
